summary.intent: Address PR review comments on the moltis/pull/585 branch, fixing three issues flagged by the Greptile bot.
summary.outcome: Two real fixes were applied (translatable continue message in websocket.js, documented tool-call threshold in schema.rs and template.rs); the third comment about template alignment was determined to be a false positive. Changes passed formatting, linting, and tests, then were committed and pushed.

repo: Greptile bot is used for automated PR review on the moltis repo and can produce false positives (flagged correct column alignment as misaligned).
The config system has both a schema.rs (doc-comments) and a template.rs (example config template) that must be kept in sync when documenting config fields.
The `>= 3` tool-call threshold gate in the config was previously undocumented.

code.finding: Continue message was a bare string literal; needed to be made translatable and phrased naturally for display in conversation history.
Tool-call threshold (>= 3) lacked a doc-comment explaining its purpose and semantics.
Template comment for tool-call threshold field also needed updating to match schema docs. Column-35 alignment of # comments is the established convention.

workflow: When a bot flags an alignment issue, verify manually with exact column counts before making changes — the diff context can be misleading.
Run Rust fmt, JS lint, and crate tests before committing even for small comment/string fixes.

friction: Greptile flagged correct template alignment as a bug, requiring manual column-by-column verification to confirm it was a false positive.
